failed_cases.description: Userinfo is preserved in full URL serialization while host authority remains host-plus-port.
failed_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, parse_url
src = 'http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f'
p = parse_url(src)
u = URL(src)
print(p['username'], p['password'], p['host'], p['port'])
print(u.username, u.password, u.get_authority())
print(u.to_text())
failed_cases.expected_output:
user pass example.com 8080
user pass example.com:8080
http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f
errors:
expected 'user pass example.com 8080
user pass example.com:8080
http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f', got 'user pass example.com 8080
user pass user:pass@example.com:8080
http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f'
failed_cases.actual:
user pass example.com 8080
user pass user:pass@example.com:8080
http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f
failed_cases.expected:
user pass example.com 8080
user pass example.com:8080
http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f

failed_cases.description: Scheme-relative and path-only URLs preserve their absent scheme/authority state through parsing and navigation.
failed_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, parse_url
for src in ['//example.com/path', 'example.com/path']:
    p = parse_url(src)
    print(src, repr(p['scheme']), repr(p['_netloc_sep']), repr(p['host']), repr(p['path']))
base = URL('https://root.test/a/b/')
print(base.navigate('//cdn.test/lib.js').to_text())
print(base.navigate('child').to_text())
failed_cases.expected_output:
//example.com/path None '//' 'example.com' '/path'
example.com/path None None None 'example.com/path'
https://cdn.test/lib.js
https://root.test/a/b/child
errors:
expected "//example.com/path None '//' 'example.com' '/path'
example.com/path None None None 'example.com/path'
https://cdn.test/lib.js
https://root.test/a/b/child", got "//example.com/path '' '//' 'example.com' '/path'
example.com/path '' '' '' 'example.com/path'
https://cdn.test/lib.js
https://root.test/a/b/child"
failed_cases.expected:
//example.com/path None '//' 'example.com' '/path'
example.com/path None None None 'example.com/path'
https://cdn.test/lib.js
https://root.test/a/b/child

failed_cases.description: IPv6 host parsing, family detection, authority serialization, and normalization agree.
failed_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, parse_url
p = parse_url('HTTP://[::1]:80/a/../p')
fam = getattr(p['family'], 'name', None)
u = URL('HTTP://[::1]:80/a/../p')
u.normalize()
print(p['host'], p['port'], fam)
print(u.host, u.get_authority())
print(u.to_text())

all_cases.description: Public names import from the single module.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, QueryParamDict, parse_url, find_all_links, URLParseError
print(URL.__name__, QueryParamDict.__name__, parse_url.__name__, find_all_links.__name__)
print(issubclass(URLParseError, ValueError))

all_cases.description: parse_url decomposes a normal absolute URL into public components.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import parse_url
p = parse_url('https://example.com:8443/a/b?x=1#frag')
print(p['scheme'], p['_netloc_sep'], p['host'], p['port'])
print(p['path'], p['query'], p['fragment'])

all_cases.description: URL exposes basic attributes and serializes unchanged input.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL
u = URL('https://example.com/a/b?x=1#frag')
print(u.scheme, u.host, u.path, u.fragment, u.port, u.uses_netloc)
print(list(u.query_params.items(multi=True)))
print(u.to_text())
print(u.get_authority())
all_cases.expected_output:
https example.com /a/b frag None True
[('x', '1')]
https://example.com/a/b?x=1#frag
example.com
all_cases.actual:
https example.com /a/b frag None True
[('x', '1')]
https://example.com/a/b?x=1#frag
example.com
all_cases.expected:
https example.com /a/b frag None True
[('x', '1')]
https://example.com/a/b?x=1#frag
example.com

all_cases.description: QueryParamDict parses repeated and bare keys without losing order.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import QueryParamDict
q = QueryParamDict.from_text('a=1&a=2&empty&b=')
print(list(q.items(multi=True)))
print(q.getlist('a'))
print(q.to_text())

all_cases.description: QueryParamDict add and assignment expose the documented multi-value API.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import QueryParamDict
q = QueryParamDict([('a','1')])
q.add('a', '2')
q['b'] = '3'
print(list(q.items(multi=True)))
print(q.getlist('a'), q.getlist('b'))

all_cases.description: URL.navigate resolves a simple relative reference.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL
u = URL('https://ex.com/a/b/c')
print(u.navigate('../d?x=1#f').to_text())

all_cases.description: normalize mutates a URL in place for common scheme, host, port, and dot-segment cases.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL
u = URL('HTTP://Example.COM:80/a/../b/%7Eme')
ret = u.normalize()
print(ret)
print(u.to_text())

all_cases.description: find_all_links extracts common HTTP(S) links from prose.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import find_all_links
links = find_all_links('go http://example.com/a and https://host/path. email x@y.com')
print([u.to_text() for u in links])

all_cases.description: Malformed URLs raise useful Python exceptions.
Userinfo is preserved in full URL serialization while host authority remains host-plus-port.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, parse_url
src = 'http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f'
p = parse_url(src)
u = URL(src)
print(p['username'], p['password'], p['host'], p['port'])
print(u.username, u.password, u.get_authority())
print(u.to_text())

http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f'

user pass user:pass@example.com:8080
http://user:pass@example.com:8080/a?x=1#f

all_cases.description: Query mutation order is reflected in URL serialization after appending and replacing repeated keys.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import QueryParamDict, URL
q = QueryParamDict([('b','2'), ('a','1'), ('a','3')])
q.add('empty', '')
q['a'] = 'last'
print(list(q.items(multi=True)))
u = URL('https://ex.test/path')
u.query_params = q
print(u.to_text())

all_cases.description: URL.from_parts output can be navigated from and keeps query-param state coherent.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, QueryParamDict
q = QueryParamDict([('a','1')]); q.add('a','2')
u = URL.from_parts('https', host='example.com', path_parts=['api','v1'], query_params=q)
print(u.to_text())
print(u.navigate('../v2/users?active=true').to_text())

all_cases.description: A normalized URL can be edited through query params and used as the base for later navigation.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL
u = URL('HTTPS://Example.com:443/app/../search?q=old&q=older#Top')
u.normalize()
u.query_params['q'] = 'new'
u.query_params.add('page', '1')
print(u.to_text())
print(u.navigate('../item/42?view=full').to_text())

all_cases.description: Extracted URL objects can be sorted, edited with query params, and serialized.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import find_all_links
text = 'A https://b.test/path?z=9 and http://a.test/root.'
links = sorted(find_all_links(text), key=lambda u: (u.host, u.path))
for idx, u in enumerate(links, 1):
    u.query_params.add('seen', str(idx))
    print(u.to_text())

all_cases.description: IPv6 host parsing, family detection, authority serialization, and normalization agree.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, parse_url
p = parse_url('HTTP://[::1]:80/a/../p')
fam = getattr(p['family'], 'name', None)
u = URL('HTTP://[::1]:80/a/../p')
u.normalize()
print(p['host'], p['port'], fam)
print(u.host, u.get_authority())
print(u.to_text())

all_cases.description: A failed parse does not corrupt later URL and query-param operations in the same process.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL, QueryParamDict, parse_url
try:
    parse_url('http://[::1')
except Exception:
    print('parse-error')
u = URL('https://ok.test/a?x=1')
u.query_params.add('y', '2')
print(u.to_text())
print(QueryParamDict.from_text('a=1&a=2').getlist('a'))

all_cases.description: Sorted query-param copies serialize predictably without mutating the original URL query order.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL
u = URL('https://ex.test/path?b=2&a=1&a=3')
sorted_q = u.query_params.sorted()
print(sorted_q.to_text())
print(u.query_params.to_text())
print(u.to_text())

all_cases.description: Normalizing a base URL before relative navigation changes the base path used for the later resolution.
all_cases.test_code:
from miniurlutils import URL
u = URL('HTTP://Example.COM:80/a/x/../b/')
u.normalize()
print(u.to_text())
print(u.navigate('../c').to_text())
